truth.papers.A5.title: Decomposition in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels
truth.papers.A5.journal: Journal of Materials Research and Technology
truth.papers.A5.paper_scope: Study austenite decomposition reactions (discontinuous precipitation, precipitation transformation, cellular transformation, spinodal decomposition) in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels at 600°C and 800°C tempering, characterize microstructures and Vickers hardness.

prediction.papers.A5.title: Decomposition in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels
prediction.papers.A5.paper_scope: Study of austenite decomposition reactions at 600°C and 800°C for different chemical compositions in Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels, characterizing discontinuous precipitation, precipitation transformation, cellular transformation, and spinodal decomposition using optical microscopy, SEM, EDS, and Vickers hardness.

prediction.processes.proc_9al10mn1c_hr.processes_no: Common initial thermomechanical processing for all cast ingots.
prediction.processes.proc_600c.description: Tempered at 600°C for 30 min, followed by water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_600c.processes_notes: Triggered discontinuous precipitation and precipitation transformation.

truth.structures.sample_9al10mn04c_600.overall: Partial discontinuous precipitation: γ grains transformed into γ+κ lamellae starting from grain boundaries; some untransformed γ band-shaped regions remain.
truth.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_600.overall_: Almost complete discontinuous precipitation: most γ transformed into γ+κ lamellar structure, possibly with some α phase; few residual γ bands survive and may contain nano-sized κ precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_600.microstr: fine lamellae in γ+κ lamellar colonies
fine lamellae, also nano-sized possible in residual γ
truth.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_800.overall_: Cellular transformation and spinodal decomposition: cellular transformation produces κ+α+residual-γ lamellar structures; spinodal decomposition gives nano-sized κ in untransformed γ grains.
truth.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_800.microstr: residual in cellular regions, spinodal decomposed grains
lamellar in cellular zones; nano-sized acicular agglomerates in spinodal zones
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn04c_600.overal: Whole γ volume undergoes multifaceted precipitation transformation: austenite matrix with carbon-depleted γ regions and globular κ-carbides; strong κ precipitation along δ/γ boundaries and some κ within ferrite.
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn04c_600.micros: globular carbides in carbon-depleted γ and along δ/γ boundaries
ferrite grains, some κ precipitation inside
at δ/γ boundaries and in carbon-depleted γ
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_600.overall: Precipitation transformation: γ+κ lamellae and alternating bands of two different γ grades (one carbon-depleted with globular κ). No lamellar structure like discontinuous precipitation; globular κ embedded in matrix.
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_600.microst: two grades: carbon-depleted with globular κ and other grade
globular carbides in carbon-depleted γ bands
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_800.overall: Complete cellular transformation: α+κ coarse lamellae (lath-like κ strips) with wide inter-lamellar spacing; scattered thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains.
truth.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_800.microst: wide, thick lath-like strips in α+κ lamellae
in α+κ lamellar colonies, wide inter-lamellar spacing
truth.structures.sample_12al20mn1c_800.overall: Full austenitic decomposition: cellular transformation into κ+α+residual-γ lamellar structure, plus spinodal decomposition in other γ grains; κ appears as wide, thick lath-like lamellae; mixed structure with δ ferrite branches extending into spinodal decomposed γ.
truth.structures.sample_12al20mn1c_800.microst: ferrite lamellae in cellular regions; δ-ferrite bands
residual γ in cellular lamellae; spinodal decomposed γ grains
wide, thick lath-like strips in cellular product; cuboidal nano-sized precipitates in spinodal zones
truth.structures.sample_12al30mn1c_800.overall: Fully spinodal decomposed austenite: nano-sized κ-carbides within γ grains; morphology: acicular-like, fine and less dense.
truth.structures.sample_12al30mn1c_800.microst: nano-sized acicular agglomerates, fine, less dense
truth.structures.sample_9al20mn1c_600.overall_: No austenite transformation detected; secondary phases along grain boundaries (possibly γ+α in 9Al-20Mn-1C and only κ in 9Al-30Mn-1C); austenite grains show parallel striations with different orientations.
truth.structures.sample_9al20mn1c_600.microstr: along grain boundaries, possible continuity with δ
γ+α or κ at grain boundaries
truth.structures.sample_9al30mn1c_600.overall_: Similar to 9Al-20Mn-1C-600: no austenite transformation, but only κ-carbides at grain boundaries; austenite grains with parallel striations.

prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn04c_600.overall: Partial discontinuous precipitation into γ + κ lamellar structures starting from grain boundaries; partial transformation only
prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn04c_600.microst: parent grains with partial lamellar transformation
prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_600.overall_: Almost fully transformed austenite via discontinuous precipitation into γ + κ fine lamellae; few γ-band-shaped regions survived; possible nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation in non-transformed austenite
prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_600.microstr: fine lamellae and possible intragranular nano-precipitates
prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_800.overall_: Cellular transformation producing α + κ + residual γ lamellar structures; spinodal decomposition in γ + nano-sized κ-carbides; thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains
prediction.structures.sample_9al10mn1c_800.microstr: intragranular within spinodally decomposed γ; lamellar in cellular transformed regions
prediction.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_600.overall: Precipitation transformation featuring fine/coarse γ + κ lamellae, carbon-depleted γ bands, and globular κ-carbides; not ordered discontinuous precipitation
prediction.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_600.microst: bands with carbon-depleted regions and globular precipitates
within carbon-depleted γ bands and along δ/γ boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_800.overall: Complete cellular transformation into α + κ coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing; scattered thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains
prediction.structures.sample_12al10mn1c_800.microst: coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing
prediction.structures.sample_12al20mn1c_800.overall: Full austenitic decomposition via cellular transformation into α + κ + residual γ lamellae along with spinodal decomposition; wide and thick lath-like κ-carbide lamellae; δ-ferrite branches inside spinodal decomposed γ grains
prediction.structures.sample_12al20mn1c_800.microst: residual lamellae and spinodally decomposed grains
branches extending into spinodal decomposed γ grains
wide and thick lath-like lamellae; cuboidal nano-sized precipitates
cuboidal nano-precipitates in spinodal regions; thick lath-like lamellae in cellular regions
prediction.structures.sample_12al30mn1c_800.overall: Fully spinodal decomposed austenite with nano-sized κ-carbides; acicular-like precipitates in finest and less dense configuration
prediction.structures.sample_12al30mn1c_800.microst: intragranular acicular-like precipitates, finest and less dense configuration
prediction.structures.sample_9al20mn1c_600.overall_: Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain; secondary phases along grain boundaries (probably δ + α); no detected austenite transformation
prediction.structures.sample_9al30mn1c_600.overall_: Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain; κ-carbides along grain boundaries; no detected austenite transformation

truth.interfaces.sample_9al10mn04c_600.interfa: Discontinuous precipitation starts from γ grain boundaries, leading to lamellar γ+κ growth.
truth.interfaces.sample_9al10mn04c_600.phase_e: γ transforms to γ+κ lamellae at grain boundaries.
truth.interfaces.sample_9al10mn1c_600.interfac: Lamellar product formed by grain boundary migration (discontinuous precipitation).
truth.interfaces.sample_9al10mn1c_600.phase_ev: Nearly complete γ transformation to γ+κ lamellae.
truth.interfaces.sample_9al10mn1c_800.interfac: Cellular transformation produces α+κ+γ lamellae; spinodal decomposition forms nano κ within γ.
truth.interfaces.sample_12al10mn04c_600.interf: Strong κ precipitation along δ/γ boundaries; globular κ in carbon-depleted γ.
truth.interfaces.sample_12al10mn04c_600.phase_: Precipitation transformation with κ at interfaces.
truth.interfaces.sample_12al10mn1c_800.interfa: Coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing.
truth.interfaces.sample_12al20mn1c_800.interfa: δ ferrite branches extend into spinodal decomposed γ.
truth.interfaces.sample_12al20mn1c_800.phase_e: Cellular transformation with spinodal decomposition, mixed interface.

prediction.interfaces.sample_9al10mn1c_600.phase_ev: Discontinuous precipitation initiated at γ grain boundaries and grew towards adjacent γ grains, forming intergranular lamellar structures.
prediction.interfaces.sample_12al10mn1c_600.phase_e: Strong κ-carbide precipitation along δ/γ boundaries and poor precipitation within ferrite; carbon-depleted γ bands with globular κ-carbides.
prediction.interfaces.sample_12al20mn1c_800.phase_e: δ-ferrite branches extend inside spinodal decomposed γ grains; cellular transformation produces α + κ lamellae.

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Microstructure observation of all alloys at different processing conditions
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Revealed austenite decomposition products such as lamellar structures, phase distributions, and grain boundary features.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.key_findi: Imaged lamellar structures, nano-sized κ-carbides, and celluar/spinodal features (Figs. 2,5,6,7,8).
truth.characterization_methods.sample_9al10mn1: Qualitative local chemical composition of phases
Table 3: Al and Mn distribution in δ, γ, κ and γ nano-κ regions; Mn enrichment in nano-κ region (mean Mn 13.4 wt%).
Table 6: Al and Mn contents in δ, γ, κ; κ shows higher Al (mean 9.4 wt%) and Mn (mean 12.3 wt%) compared to γ.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_12al20mn: Table 7: Al and Mn in δ, γ, κ; κ enriched in Al (10.3-11.9 wt%) and Mn (21.9-25.5 wt%).
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.purpose: Measure hardness, 5 kg load, 5 indentations per sample
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.key_findi: Hardness values reported in Tables 2,4,5,8,9; correlated with microstructure (discontinuous precipitation, cellular transformation, spinodal decomposition).
truth.characterization_methods.idx_6.purpose: Assess thermodynamic stability of phases at different temperatures
truth.characterization_methods.idx_6.key_findi: Figures 9 and 12 show phase fractions vs. temperature; used to explain austenite transformation driving forces.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_9al10mn1: In 9Al-10Mn-1C tempered at 600°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 7.8-8.7, Mn 9.7-10.0), γ (Al 7.6-8.0, Mn 9.9-10.7), κ (Al 8.0-8.3, Mn 10.2-10.5), γ nano-κ (Al 7.8-8.1, Mn 13.2-13.6). Table 3.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_12al10mn: Random-like transformation with fine/coarse γ + κ lamellae, carbon-depleted γ bands, and globular κ-carbides; not ordered discontinuous precipitation. Fig. 4.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_9al10mn1: In 9Al-10Mn-1C tempered at 800°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 8.1-8.7, Mn 7.4-9.1), γ (Al 6.8-8.7, Mn 11.1-12.3), κ (Al 8.6-10.0, Mn 10.1-13.8). Table 6.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_12al10mn: Complete cellular transformation into α + κ coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing; scattered thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains. Fig. 5(b).
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_12al20mn: In 12Al-20Mn-1C tempered at 800°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 11.0-13.6, Mn 15.2-20.8), γ (Al 5.7-9.9, Mn 20.3-27.6), κ (Al 10.3-11.9, Mn 21.9-25.5). Table 7.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_12al30mn: Phase morphology analysis of spinodal decomposition
Fully spinodal decomposed austenite with nano-sized acicular-like κ-carbides in finest and less dense configuration. Fig. 5(d), Fig. 8(e,f).
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_10.purpose: Bulk hardness measurement across alloys and conditions
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_10.key_find: 5 kg load, 5 indentations per sample; outliers excluded via Minitab18. Hardness values reported for hot-rolled, 600°C tempered, and 800°C tempered conditions. Tables 2, 4, 5, 8, 9.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Qualitatively consistent with observed austenite decomposition trends.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: predict phase stability and fraction versus temperature
Used to explain why austenite transforms at certain tempering temperatures based on driving force.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Inter-lamellar spacing influences hardness: fine lamellae with narrow spacing (e.g., 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C) give higher hardness (439 HV) compared to coarse lamellae with wide spacing (e.g., 12Al-10Mn-1C at 800°C, 399 HV).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Spinodal decomposition produces different nano-κ morphologies depending on composition: acicular agglomerated (9Al-10Mn-1C), dense cuboidal (12Al-20Mn-1C), fine acicular less dense (12Al-30Mn-1C).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: In 9Al-20Mn-1C and 9Al-30Mn-1C at 600°C, no austenite transformation was observed; hardness increase attributed possibly to solid solution strengthening or very fine nano-κ.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Austenite stability is influenced by Mn content and tempering temperature: higher Mn stabilizes γ, requiring spinodal decomposition over cellular transformation at 800°C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Discontinuous precipitation occurs at 600°C in medium Mn (10%), high Al (9%), high C (1%) combinations; requires grain boundary migration.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Cellular transformation developed at 800°C between 9-12% Al; spinodal decomposition becomes dominant at higher Mn (30%) and 1% C due to balanced driving force.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Hot-rolled microstructure not characterized in detail; only hardness values provided for comparison.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Optical micrographs in Fig. 5 show mixed structures of cellular and spinodal decomposition; SEM images show nano-κ details.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Table 9 compares inter-lamellar spacing and hardness for 12Al-10Mn-1C 800°C (wide spacing) and 9Al-10Mn-1C 600°C (narrow spacing).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The paper suggests the best balance of hardness and drawability is achieved in 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C via discontinuous precipitation with fine lamellae.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Phase volume fraction diagrams (Figs. 9 and 12) illustrate thermodynamic gradients for austenite stability versus temperature, but the computational method, software, or thermodynamic database used to generate them is not specified in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: 9Al-20Mn-1C and 9Al-30Mn-1C tempered at 600°C exhibited hardening (359 HV and 330 HV respectively) without detected austenite transformation; solid solution strengthening of carbon and/or very fine nano κ-carbides are hypothesized (Table 8, Fig. 10).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain were observed in 9Al-20Mn-1C and 9Al-30Mn-1C at 600°C (Fig. 10), requiring further investigation to understand their nature.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Inter-lamellar spacing was characterized qualitatively as narrow for 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C and wide for 12Al-10Mn-1C at 800°C, correlating with hardening response (Table 9, Fig. 2 vs Fig. 5).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Figure 14 shows δ-ferrite branches extending inside spinodal decomposed γ grains in 12Al-20Mn-1C at 800°C, a phenomenon requiring further studies to be properly understood.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The best microstructure for combined hardness and expected tensile ductility is suggested to be 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C, where carbides obtained by discontinuous precipitation have narrow inter-lamellar spacing and the highest hardness value (439 HV).
